subject: ARTIFACT
text: TRANSCRIBED from DOC-009 §18.1 on 2026-07-16, replacing 'UNSPECIFIED in BOTH DOC-002 and DOC-007 ... do not fabricate. OPEN ITEM.' That note was TRUE about DOC-002/DOC-007 and WRONG as a conclusion: it searched the meaning doc and the wire doc, never the STORAGE doc. DOC-009 §18.1 defines the artifacts table in full, and its primary key is 'references professional_work_objects(id)' — so at the ratified persistence plane the Artifact IS a Professional Work Object, carrying the envelope's semanticVersion. These 11 fields are that table, column-for-column; nothing here is designed. NOT ratified at the WIRE boundary: DOC-007 gives ARTIFACT no interface, no §5.2 id prefix, no command and no event, while THREE of its field types (outputArtifactIds, ArtifactReference, ArtifactRequirement) point at it — a dangling reference DOC-009 resolves and DOC-007 has not caught up to (§16 item 6, first-slice). Value domains deliberately left as string: DOC-009 types artifactType/status/securityClassification/retentionClass as bare 'text not null' with no CHECK constraint and no enum anywhere in the corpus. Inventing enum members here would be the fabrication the original note rightly forbade.

subject: RESOLVED 2026-07-16 — ARTIFACT's fields were defined all along, in DOC-009 §18.1
text: This item read: 'ARTIFACT is one of the 17 ProfessionalWorkObjectType values but neither DOC-002 nor DOC-007 defines an ArtifactObject interface/schema ... Fields are to-be-designed; do not fabricate.' Every clause about DOC-002 and DOC-007 is true. The conclusion was false: the search stopped at the meaning doc and the wire doc and never opened the STORAGE doc. DOC-009 §18.1 'create table artifacts' defines all 11 columns, and its PK 'references professional_work_objects(id)' makes the Artifact a Professional Work Object with an envelope semanticVersion — which §18.3 then versions under supersession. The fields are now TRANSCRIBED from that table, not designed. WHAT REMAINS OPEN is narrower and belongs to DOC-007, not to the object: no ARTIFACT interface, no §5.2 id prefix ('art' is authored here; DOC-004 §… writes 'artifact_01J...'), no command, no event — even though outputArtifactIds/ArtifactReference/ArtifactRequirement all point at it. GENERALIZE THIS: 'unspecified — do not fabricate' notes in this vocab were written against DOC-002+DOC-007 only. DOC-009 is a ratified source and must be checked before any such note is trusted; the same blind spot produced a false blocker on §16 item 23 (execution_attempts, which DOC-009 §10.4 likewise defines in full).

subject: 6 objects lack a DOC-007 serialized field contract
text: AMENDED 2026-07-16: ARTIFACT is no longer sourceless — DOC-009 §18.1 defines it (see the resolved item above). It still lacks a DOC-007 SERIALIZED contract, so it stays in this list, but the gap is now wire-only, not total. The other five remain DOC-002-prose-only; each should be re-checked against DOC-009's storage schema before being called unspecified. ORIGINAL: OBLIGATION, CONSTRAINT, DECOMPOSITION_CONTRACT, RECOMPOSITION_CONTRACT, RUNTIME_BINDING (and ARTIFACT) have no field-level definition in the latest serialized contract (DOC-007). CONSTRAINT + RUNTIME_BINDING have schema FILES listed in DOC-007 §3 but no prose; the others have neither. All are sourced from DOC-002 prose and need serialization before codegen. Their enums (ObligationType/Strength/Status, ConstraintType/Strength/Status, CoverageType, DecompositionContractStatus, RecompositionContractStatus, AuthorizationStatus, StepType/State) exist but have no wired consumer field yet.
